contentBlocks.title: When do you need to convert hectares to acres?
contentBlocks.html:
Hectare (ha) is a metric area unit equal to 10,000 m² (100 m × 100 m). Acre is an imperial unit equal to 4,046.86 m². The conversion: 1 hectare = 2.47105 acres.

This conversion is essential for international real estate, farming, forestry, and land management. US and UK property listings use acres; most other countries use hectares.

Size reference: a soccer field is about 0.7 ha (1.7 acres). A standard US city block is about 0.8 ha (2 acres).

All calculations run locally in your browser — nothing is sent to any server.

items.title: 3. Copy or reverse
items.description: Click Copy result or use the Reverse button to convert in the opposite direction.

contentBlocks.title: How does hectares to acres conversion work?
contentBlocks.html:
1 hectare = 10,000 m². 1 acre = 4,046.8564224 m². Therefore, 1 ha = 10,000/4,046.856 = 2.47105 acres.

To convert hectares to acres, multiply by 2.47105. To convert acres to hectares, multiply by 0.404686.

Quick estimate: 1 hectare ≈ 2.5 acres. Multiply hectares by 2.5 for a rough conversion.

contentBlocks.title: Practical tips
contentBlocks.html:
• Soccer field: approximately 0.7 ha = 1.7 acres.
  • Average US farm: 180 ha = 445 acres.
  • 1 km² = 100 ha = 247 acres.
  • Central Park (NYC): 341 ha = 843 acres.

items.question: How big is 1 hectare?
items.answer: 1 hectare = 10,000 m² = a square 100 m on each side. About 1.4 soccer fields.

items.question: How big is 1 acre?
items.answer: 1 acre = 4,047 m². Approximately 0.7 of a soccer field, or about 16 tennis courts.

items.question: What is the quick way to convert ha to acres?
items.answer: Multiply hectares by 2.5 for a quick estimate. Example: 10 ha × 2.5 = 25 acres (exact: 24.7 acres).

items.question: Are calculations done locally?
items.answer: Yes. All calculations run in your browser. No data is sent to any server.
